AIM
Study of the effect of aqueous extract of Lactuca sativa (AEL) on the level of the stress biomarkers in the blood serum of Wistar rats.


MATERIALS AND METHODS
The effect of AEL on the level of pro-inflammatory cytokines IL-6, IL-1β and anti-inflammatory cytokine IL-10 in the blood serum of Wistar rats with forced immobilization stress was studied.


RESULTS
The experiment showed that the level of pro-inflammatory cytokines IL-6 and IL-1β in the blood serum of Wistar rats with forced immobilization stress decreased after intragastric administration of AEL.


CONCLUSION
AEL exerted an anti-inflammatory effect in Wistar rats with forced immobilization stress. .

The aim of the study was to analyze the epidemiological data of patients with femoral neck fractures in a county hospital in the Alentejo region of Portugal, and to compare the incidence of the disease with national and international data.


MATERIALS AND METHODS
A retrospective study was conducted of 121 patients with femoral neck fractures in the period 1996-2000.


RESULTS
Average age was 75.7 years, 73.2% were female, and 84.4% were admitted because of a traumatic event. The incidence of femoral neck fractures was 13.0% when all the fractures of the femur were taken into account, and 5.3% when the fractures of the femoral neck alone were considered. The incidence in men was 17.2% and 10.3% in women. The incidence in the elderly was 13.7% when those aged 65 years and over were considered, and 11.9% when those aged 75 years and over were considered.

# Introduction
Electroencephalography (EEG) is a non-invasive technique that allows the electrical activity of the brain to be measured and recorded. EEG represents the electric activity of the cortical neurons, and it is usually recorded by placing the electrodes on the scalp. Increase or decrease of brain electrical activity are considered as the main neurological sign of brain damage, such as in the case of ischemia, infarction, hemorrhage or any other type of lesion. EEG is used for the assessment of brain function, diagnosis of epileptic seizures or other neurological disorders, as well as for diagnosis and monitoring of patients with coma (1-5).
EEG is considered a gold standard examination, and it is used to monitor patients during surgery

The differential drug-induced causes include clozapine, paliperidone, haloperidol, and quetiapine.
Next, to determine the underlying cause, you must consider the duration of the symptoms, the patient's past medical history, and the patient's psychiatric history. The patient's history of diabetes mellitus raises the concern for neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS), but the symptoms of NMS typically present after 2 weeks of neuroleptic administration. The patient's past history of schizophrenia and the patient's current use of clozapine support the hypothesis of NMS.
When considering the duration of the patient's symptoms, it is important to remember that drug-induced NMS typically presents within 2 weeks of neuroleptic administration. In the case of clozapine, this window is shorter, as it typically presents within 1 week of clozapine administration. The patient's history of diabetes mellitus is a clear risk factor for NMS, but it is not necessary to have a history of diabetes.
In the case of NMS, the clinical presentation differs by the length of time the patient has been on the medication. In cases of recent initiation of clozapine, the patient may present with the classic picture of NMS, which includes muscle rigidity, fever, and dysphagia. In this case, the patient's medical history of diabetes mellitus could make NMS more likely. In cases of ongoing use of clozapine, the patient may present with one of many other symptoms, including dysphagia, fever, muscle rigidity, agitation, and confusion.

Locoregional Flap Reconstruction in Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
Abstract Background The purpose of this study was to evaluate the results of locoregional flap surgery for the reconstruction of head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) defects and to compare them with free flap surgery. Methods We reviewed the medical records of patients who underwent primary resection of HNSCC in our hospital between January 2000 and December 2012. Results Among 288 patients, 262 patients underwent locoregional flap reconstruction and 26 underwent free flap reconstruction. Locoregional flap reconstruction was performed in 25.5% of patients with tongue cancer, 58.2% of patients with oral cavity cancer, 25.3% of patients with maxillary sinus cancer, and 33.9% of patients with nasal cavity cancer. The most commonly used locoregional flaps were temporalis myofascial flap (25.4%), pectoralis major musculocutaneous flap (18.7%), and nasolabial flap (12.2%). The overall complication rate was 10.3%. The complication rate was significantly higher in free flap reconstruction (23.1%) than in locoregional flap reconstruction (6.4%) (p < 0.001). The overall survival rate was significantly lower in patients with free flap reconstruction (68.5%) than in those with locoregional flap reconstruction (85.6%) (p = 0.008). Conclusion Locoregional flap surgery can be used as an alternative to free flap surgery in selected HNSCC patients. .

Aqueous-Phase Synthesis of 3D-Printed Chitosan-Based Scaffolds for Tissue Regeneration



# Introduction
Tissue engineering is a multidisciplinary field that aims at repairing and regenerating damaged tissues or organs by the use of cells, scaffolds, and growth factors   Tissue engineering, Langer  . The application of three-dimensional (3D) scaffolds is essential for tissue regeneration. Scaffolds provide a structural support system to allow cell proliferation, migration, and differentiation. Ideally, scaffolds should provide a suitable environment for cell attachment and proliferation, be biocompatible, biodegradable, and have a suitable rate of degradation that is able to support tissue regeneration, and be cost-effective.
Scaffolds can be produced from a variety of materials such as polymers, ceramics, metals, and composites. Scaffolds can be synthesized using different methods such as physical, chemical, and biological synthesis methods. The physical methods are usually referred to as the conventional methods of fabrication. These methods are frequently used to fabricate scaffolds with limited porosity and rough surfaces. A wide variety of chemical methodologies have been developed in the past decades to fabricate scaffolds with high porosity and an interconnected pore structure. These methods include freeze-drying, gas foaming, modified phase separation, and emulsion freeze-drying, as well as gas foaming, polymer precipitation, solvent casting, and thermally induced phase separation   Fabrication of porous scaffolds for tissue engineering, Lu     Biomaterials in bone and cartilage regeneration, Jacob  . Biological scaffolds can be produced by the use of the extracellular matrix (ECM) or by the use of 3D printing techniques. This scaffold fabrication technique is gaining much interest in the tissue engineering field, as it permits the fabrication of scaffolds with precise control of the morphology and composition of the scaffold   Three-dimensional (3D) printing of scaffolds for tissue regeneration applications, Do

# Introduction
The causative agent of COVID-19, SARS-CoV-2, is a novel coronavirus that emerged in late 2019 in Wuhan, China. SARS-CoV-2 is an enveloped, positive-sense, single-stranded RNA virus with a large genome of about 30 kb. It belongs to the genus Betacoronavirus, which also includes the coronaviruses that caused the 2002/2004 SARS outbreak and the 2012 MERS outbreak   The coronavirus pandemic in five powerful charts, Callaway  . On March 11, 2020, the World Health Organization (WHO) declared the COVID-19 outbreak a pandemic .
The Coronaviridae family is composed of four genera, alpha, beta, gamma, and delta, of which beta and alpha are responsible for the vast majority of human infections   Coronaviruses: an overview of their replication and pathogenesis, Fehr  .
SARS-CoV-2 is a betacoronavirus and is the seventh known human coronavirus, belonging to the Betacoronavirus genus, which includes the SARS-CoV and the MERS-CoV.
The SARS-CoV-2 genome encodes four structural proteins: spike (S), envelope (E), membrane (M), and nucleocapsid (N), as well as 16 non-structural proteins (NSPs) and nine accessory proteins. conversation.response:
Relationship between the absorption coefficient and the reflectance phantom.
A body of information is available that relates the reflectance phantom to the absorption coefficient of tissue. The relationship between these two quantities is addressed in this paper. The reflectance phantom is defined as the fraction of incident light that is reflected. The absorption coefficient is defined as the fraction of incident light that is absorbed by the tissue. One can then relate these two quantities by calculating the ratio of the absorption coefficient to the reflectance phantom. It was found that the ratio of the absorption coefficient to the reflectance phantom has a maximum value of 1, and that the ratio of the absorption coefficient to the reflectance phantom is equal to the reflectance phantom when the absorption coefficient is unity. The use of the vertical needle to obtain a better fixation of the laser fibre in the posterior fossa in endoscopic laser surgery of cranial nerves
Surgical procedures that involve a complex anatomical structure such as the posterior fossa and the cranial nerves require an accurate and safe positioning of the laser fibre to avoid inadvertent damage to these delicate structures. To obtain the best possible positioning of the laser fibre in the lateral recess of the upper cervical spine in endoscopic laser surgery of cranial nerves, we have developed a novel method using the vertical needle. The vertical needle is a needle with a vertical axis. It can be inserted into the lateral recess of the upper cervical spine with a known depth and can serve as a guide for the laser fibre. The vertical needle was placed into the lateral recess of the upper cervical spine under endoscopic visual control. The laser fibre was then inserted into the lateral recess of the upper cervical spine with the aid of the vertical needle. In this manner, we could obtain a stable and safe position of the laser fibre in the lateral recess of the upper cervical spine. The vertical needle may be a useful guide to obtaining a more stable position of the laser fibre in endoscopic laser surgery of cranial nerves. <gh_stars>0

The patient's history and symptoms were concerning for toxic ingestion. His initial vital signs were remarkable for tachycardia (110 beats per minute), but otherwise he was euvolemic and normothermic.

Changes in the metabolism of aortic smooth muscle cells in response to phorbol ester and constant pH.
The growth of cultured aortic smooth muscle cells is stimulated by incubation at pH 7.0. The effect is partially mimicked by the tumor-promoting agent phorbol myristate acetate (PMA). The purpose of the present study was to determine the effects of chronic exposure to phorbol ester and constant pH on cellular metabolism. Both PMA and constant pH inhibited the incorporation of amino acids into protein, but not the steady-state protein level. The PMA-induced decrease in protein synthesis was not accompanied by a change in the total cellular free amino acid pool. PMA stimulated the incorporation of glucose into glycogen and inhibited the incorporation of glucose into hexose phosphates, but did not affect the glucose-6-phosphate (G-6-P) level. PMA also reduced the rate of glucose oxidation, as indicated by the decrease in the rate of oxygen consumption and the accumulation of lactate. PMA-induced changes in the incorporation of glucose into glycogen and hexose phosphates were blocked by cycloheximide. The incorporation of glucose into glycogen was further increased by the addition of insulin to the medium. Furthermore, PMA did not alter the activity of hexokinase or the rate of phosphorylation of fructose-6-phosphate (F-6-P). The results suggest that PMA decreases the activity of one or more enzymes in the pathway from hexose phosphates to glycogen. PMA also decreased the incorporation of glucose into lipids. The differences between the effects of PMA and constant pH on the cellular metabolism are discussed. The heart-brain syndrome.

conversation.response:
Adrenal androgens in the female.
The adrenal cortex of the normal adult female is the source of a small amount of androgen, which is converted to oestrogens by the peripheral tissues. Three androgenic compounds may be isolated from urine in normal women: dehydroepiandrosterone, androstenedione and androsterone. While androstenedione and androsterone may be mainly of adrenal origin, dehydroepiandrosterone is also produced by peripheral tissues although its adrenal origin is undisputed. The androgenic compounds are not significantly altered in the postmenopausal age. In order to achieve a good correlation between clinical and biochemical findings, it is important to pay attention to factors which may influence the adrenal androgen secretion. 17β-Estradiol Alleviates Ethanol-Induced Apoptosis of Bovine Granulosa Cells by Regulating the PI3K/Akt Signaling Pathway

The aim of this study was to investigate the mechanism of apoptosis of bovine granulosa cells (GCs) induced by ethanol and the role of 17β-estradiol in the process. The bovine GCs were treated with ethanol at different concentrations. Propidium iodide (PI) and Hoechst 33342 were used to stain the nuclei, and the apoptosis rate was analyzed by flow cytometry. Reverse transcription-polymerase chain reaction (RT-PCR) was used to detect the expression levels of estrogen receptor α (ERα), phosphatidylinositol 3-kinase (PI3K), Akt, and caspase 3. The results showed that 100 mM ethanol significantly increased cell apoptosis and that the expression levels of ERα, PI3K, and Akt mRNA were significantly reduced, indicating that ethanol induces apoptosis of bovine GCs and that the PI3K/Akt signaling pathway is involved.
